Class SVGNumber

java.lang.Object
org.tquadrat.foundation.javadoc.internal.foundation.svg.type.SVGNumber
Direct Known Subclasses:
SVGNumber.SVGDegree, SVGNumber.SVGMillimeter, SVGNumber.SVGPercent, SVGNumber.SVGPixel, SVGNumber.SVGUserUnitValue
@ClassVersion(sourceVersion="$Id: SVGNumber.java 1133 2024-05-10 21:43:08Z tquadrat $") @API(status=STABLE, since="0.0.5") public sealed class SVGNumber extends Object permits SVGNumber.SVGDegree, SVGNumber.SVGMillimeter, SVGNumber.SVGPercent, SVGNumber.SVGPixel, SVGNumber.SVGUserUnitValue
A numeric type with a unit as used by various SVG attributes.
Author:
Thomas Thrien - thomas.thrien@tquadrat.org
Version:
$Id: SVGNumber.java 1133 2024-05-10 21:43:08Z tquadrat $
Since:
0.0.5

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static final class 
    SVGNumber.SVGDegree
    An SVG degrees type.
    static final class 
    SVGNumber.SVGMillimeter
    An SVG millimeter type.
    static final class 
    SVGNumber.SVGPercent
    An SVG per cent type.
    static final class 
    SVGNumber.SVGPixel
    An SVG pixel type.
    static final class 
    SVGNumber.SVGUserUnitValue
    An SVG user unit type; usually this is the same as SVGNumber.SVGPixel, but without a unit specifier.

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private final boolean
    m_IsInteger
    Flag that indicates whether the type is an integer (int) - as in opposite to a double).
    private final boolean
    m_IsNegative
    Flag that indicates whether the type is a negative number.
    private final boolean
    m_IsZero
    Flag that indicates whether the type is zero.
    private final String
    m_Value

  • Field Details

    • m_IsInteger

      private final boolean m_IsInteger
      Flag that indicates whether the type is an integer (int) - as in opposite to a double). There it does not matter that the decimal places of a double are effectively zero.

    • m_IsNegative

      private final boolean m_IsNegative
      Flag that indicates whether the type is a negative number.

    • m_IsZero

      private final boolean m_IsZero
      Flag that indicates whether the type is zero.

    • m_Value

      private final String m_Value
      The type.

  • Constructor Details

    • SVGNumber

      public SVGNumber(double value, SVGUnit unit)
      Creates a new SVGNumber instance.
      Parameters:
      value - The type.
      unit - The unit.

    • SVGNumber

      public SVGNumber(long value, SVGUnit unit)
      Creates a new SVGNumber instance.
      Parameters:
      value - The type.
      unit - The unit.

  • Method Details

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object obj)
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object

    • isInteger

      public final boolean isInteger()
      Returns a flag that indicates whether the type is an integer (int) - as in opposite to a double). There it does not matter that the decimal places of a double are effectively zero.
      Returns:
      true if the type is an integer, false otherwise.

    • isNegative

      public final boolean isNegative()
      Returns a flag that indicates whether the type is a negative number.
      Returns:
      true if the type is less than zero, false otherwise.

    • isZero

      public final boolean isZero()
      Returns a flag that indicates whether the type is zero.
      Returns:
      true if the type is 0 (or 0.0), false otherwise.

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

    • value

      public final String value()
      Return the type with the unit for this instance.
      Returns:
      The type with unit.
